message:
feat(filesize): add human-readable file size and transfer speed formatting

New filesize module provides Python Rich-style formatting for byte sizes,
supporting both binary (1024-based: KiB, MiB, GiB) and decimal (1000-based:
kB, MB, GB) unit systems.

Core types and functions:

SizeUnit enum:
- SizeUnit::Binary (1024-based: KiB, MiB, GiB, TiB, PiB, EiB, ZiB, YiB)
- SizeUnit::Decimal (1000-based: kB, MB, GB, TB, PB, EB, ZB, YB)

Size formatting:
- format_size(i64, SizeUnit, precision) - full control
- decimal(u64) / binary(u64) - convenience with 1 decimal place
- decimal_with_precision() / binary_with_precision() - custom precision
- Handles negative values (for size deltas)
- Graceful fallback to "bytes" for small values

Speed formatting:
- format_speed(f64, SizeUnit, precision) - bytes/sec to human readable
- decimal_speed() / binary_speed() - convenience functions
- Appends /s suffix to unit (e.g., "1.5 MB/s", "1.0 MiB/s")

All functions exported via prelude for ergonomic usage.
Comprehensive test coverage for edge cases and large values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
